title: Do QR codes expire? The honest answer
description: Static QR codes never expire; dynamic ones can be switched off. Here's the difference, why it decides whether your printed code survives, and how to tell which one you have.
outline: Answer in one line: static codes can't expire, dynamic codes can be turned off.
Why static is permanent (content lives in the pattern).
Why dynamic depends on a redirect server that a company controls.
The trial trap: 'free' codes that are really dynamic codes on a 14-day timer.
How to check which one you have (decode it; look for a generator domain).
What to do if you already printed one that's dynamic.

title: Your QR code stopped working — here's how to fix it
description: If a printed QR code suddenly hits a paywall or a dead page, it was almost certainly a dynamic code whose trial lapsed. Here's what happened and the fastest ways to recover.
outline: First, diagnose: scan it and read what it actually points to.
If it lands on a paywall/upgrade page: it's a lapsed dynamic-code trial.
Option 1: pay the ransom once, then migrate before renewal.
Option 2: replace with a free static code (if the destination is fixed).
Option 3: move to a dynamic code whose redirect never expires.
How to avoid it next time (the 5-second test).

title: Static vs dynamic QR codes: which do you actually need?
description: Static codes are free forever and can't be edited; dynamic codes are editable and trackable but depend on a server. A plain decision guide, with the one question that settles it.
outline: The one-line difference and the one-question decision.
Static: how it works, when it's the right call, why it's free.
Dynamic: editing after print + analytics, and the catch (a middleman).
A short comparison table.
The trap to avoid: dynamic sold as 'free'.
Recommendation by use case (menu, flyer, wifi, campaign).

title: Free QR code generator with no expiration — how it actually works
description: A genuinely free QR code needs no account and never expires, because the data is encoded in the pattern itself. Here's how to make one and how to spot the fake-free ones.
primaryKeyword: free qr code generator no expiration
outline: What 'free forever' actually requires (a static code, no account).
Why the pattern-encoded data can't be switched off.
How the fake-free generators work (trial-wrapped dynamic codes).
Step by step: make a permanent free code.
When you'd legitimately want to pay (editing, analytics) and what to demand.

title: How to make a QR code for a restaurant menu (free, no expiration)
description: A step-by-step guide to a menu QR code that won't hold your printing hostage: static vs dynamic for menus, the right size and error correction, and how to test before you print.
outline: Static or dynamic for a menu? (dynamic if the menu changes, but only with a no-shutoff guarantee).
Step by step: create it, point it at your menu page or PDF.
Size and placement on table tents and printed menus.
Error correction level for restaurant conditions (spills, wear).
Test before printing 500 (the 30-second check).
Avoid the trial trap that paywalls your table tents.

title: Is qr-code-generator.com free? What the plan really means
description: The most-marketed QR generator advertises 'free' codes. Here's what actually happens after you print one, what it costs, and the honest alternative.
outline: Short answer: static previews are free; the useful codes are trial-wrapped dynamic codes.
What the reviews report (1.6 star Trustpilot, ~£130/yr, auto-renewal).
The mechanism that makes 'free' turn into a bill.
How to use it safely if you already have (the 5-second test).
The alternative: static free forever + a covenant on dynamic codes.
